title: Apply authorized decisions through public LEDGER operations
description: Execute a replay-safe authorized Classification Apply Run through public LEDGER assignment or correction operations and record one terminal result per requested transaction. Implements G3 and UC-CLASSIFY-003.
rationale: Agents must recover from retries and partial progress without duplicating or silently changing category history.
story_so_that: Ledger category history changes exactly as authorized and every result is recoverable
acceptance_criteria: Given an authorized selection whose evaluation, projection, category, normalization, and rule-set fingerprints are current, when apply starts, then CLASSIFY completes preflight before the first LEDGER mutation.
Given any stale or incompatible item detected during preflight, when apply starts, then CLASSIFY rejects the whole requested selection before the first LEDGER mutation.

Given an authorized uncategorized active transaction and active category, when its item is submitted, then CLASSIFY invokes only the public LEDGER category-assignment operation with a stable per-transaction idempotency identity.

Given an already categorized transaction, when a correction is explicitly selected, then CLASSIFY requires a transaction-specific owner reason and never includes that correction in exact-rule broad apply.
Given an apply request, when processing terminates, then every requested transaction has one structured result of applied, already-applied, rejected, failed, or unresolved.
Given the same apply identity and semantically identical request, when apply is retried, then CLASSIFY returns the prior terminal item results and creates no second category mutation.
Given an apply identity reused with different selection, category, mode, fingerprint, or reason, when apply is retried, then CLASSIFY returns a stable conflict and preserves the original results.
Given interruption between item submissions, when the same apply run is resumed, then completed items reconcile from durable results and processing continues only from the exact unresolved frontier.
security_criteria: Apply logs and diagnostics contain safe run, item, and error identifiers only, never descriptions, amounts, category payloads, or serialized LEDGER requests.
